fabric-command-api-v2.description: Callback for when client commands are registered to the dispatcher. To register some commands, you would register an event listener and implement the callback. See ClientCommands for more details and an example

fabric-command-api-v2.function: void register(CommandDispatcher<CommandSourceStack> dispatcher, CommandBuildContext buildContext, Commands.CommandSelection selection);

fabric-creative-tab-api-v1.description: Returns the modify output event for a specific creative mode tab. This uses the tab's ID and is suitable for modifying a modded creative mode tab that might not exist
Modifies the creative mode tab output

fabric-dimensions-v1.function: void modifyDimensionAttributes(Holder<DimensionType> dimension, EnvironmentAttributeMap.Builder attributes, HolderLookup.Provider registries);
fabric-dimensions-v1.description: This event can be used to modify the environment attributes of a dimension. The main use case is to add modded attributes to vanilla or modded dimensions

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowElytraFlight(LivingEntity entity);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event to check if elytra flight (both through normal and custom elytras) is allowed. All listeners need to return true to allow the entity to fly, otherwise elytra flight will be blocked/stopped

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean useCustomElytra(LivingEntity entity, boolean tickElytra);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event to grant elytra flight to living entities when some condition is met. Will be called when players try to start elytra flight by pressing space in mid-air, and every tick for all flying living entities to check if elytra flight is still allowed. Items that wish to enable custom elytra flight when worn in the chest equipment slot can add the GLIDER component to an item

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: Player.@Nullable BedSleepingProblem allowSleep(Player player, BlockPos sleepingPos);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event that checks whether a player can start to sleep in a bed-like block. This event only applies to sleeping using startSleepInBed(BlockPos). Note: Please use the more detailed event ALLOW_NEARBY_MONSTERS if it matches your use case! This helps with mod compatibility. If this event returns a BedSleepingProblem, it is used as the return value of startSleepInBed(BlockPos) and sleeping fails. A null return value means that the player will start sleeping. When this event is called, all vanilla sleeping checks have already succeeded, i.e. this event is used in addition to vanilla checks. The more detailed event ALLOW_NEARBY_MONSTERS is also checked before this event

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void onStartSleeping(LivingEntity entity, BlockPos sleepingPos);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether a player can start sleeping in a bed-like block

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void onStopSleeping(LivingEntity entity, BlockPos sleepingPos);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when an entity starts to sleep

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: EventResult allowBed(LivingEntity entity, BlockPos sleepingPos, BlockState state, boolean vanillaResult);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when an entity stops sleeping and wakes up

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: EventResult allowSleepTime(Player player, BlockPos sleepingPos, boolean vanillaResult);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether a block is a valid bed for the entity. Non-PASS passing return values cancel further callbacks

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: EventResult allowNearbyMonsters(Player player, BlockPos sleepingPos, boolean vanillaResult);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether the current time of day is valid for sleeping. Non-PASS passing return values cancel further callbacks

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowResettingTime(Player player);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether a player can sleep when monsters are nearby. Non-PASS passing return values cancel further callbacks

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: Direction modifySleepDirection(LivingEntity entity, BlockPos sleepingPos, @Nullable Direction sleepingDirection);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether a sleeping player counts into skipping the current day and resetting the time to 0

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowSettingSpawn(Player player, BlockPos sleepingPos);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Modifies or provides a sleeping direction for a block. The sleeping direction is where a player's head is pointing when they're sleeping

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean setBedOccupationState(LivingEntity entity, BlockPos sleepingPos, BlockState bedState, boolean occupied);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether a player's spawn can be set when sleeping

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: Vec3 modifyWakeUpPosition(LivingEntity entity, BlockPos sleepingPos, BlockState bedState, @Nullable Vec3 wakeUpPos);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Sets the occupation state of a bed block

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterKilledOtherEntity(ServerLevel level, Entity entity, LivingEntity killedEntity, DamageSource damageSource);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event that is called after an entity is directly responsible for killing another entity

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterChangeLevel(Entity originalEntity, Entity newEntity, ServerLevel origin, ServerLevel destination);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event which is called after an entity has been moved to a different level. All entities are copied to the destination and the old entity removed. This event does not apply to the ServerPlayer since players are physically moved to the new level instead of being copied over. A mod may use this event for reference cleanup if it is tracking an entity's current level

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterChangeLevel(ServerPlayer player, ServerLevel origin, ServerLevel destination);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called after an entity has been recreated at the destination when being moved to a different level. Note this event is not called if the entity is a ServerPlayer. AfterPlayerChange should be used to track when a player has changed levels

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowDamage(LivingEntity entity, DamageSource source, float amount);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event that is called when a living entity is going to take damage. This is fired from hurtServer, before armor or any other mitigation are applied. Mods can cancel this to prevent the damage entirely

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterDamage(LivingEntity entity, DamageSource source, float baseDamageTaken, float damageTaken, boolean blocked);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when a living entity is going to take damage. Can be used to cancel the damage entirely. The amount corresponds to the "incoming" damage amount, before armor and other mitigations have been applied

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowDeath(LivingEntity entity, DamageSource damageSource, float damageAmount);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called after a living entity took damage, unless they were killed. The base damage taken is given as damage taken before armor or enchantments are applied, but after other effects like shields are applied

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterDeath(LivingEntity entity, DamageSource damageSource);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when a living entity takes fatal damage (before totems of undying can take effect)

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void onConversion(Mob previous, Mob converted, ConversionParams conversionContext);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when a living entity dies. The death cannot be canceled at this point

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void copyFromPlayer(ServerPlayer oldPlayer, ServerPlayer newPlayer, boolean alive);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event that is called when the data from an old player is copied to a new player. This event is called after the old player is removed and untracked, but before the new player is added and tracked. Mods may use this event to copy old player data to a new player

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterRespawn(ServerPlayer oldPlayer, ServerPlayer newPlayer, boolean alive);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when player data is copied to a new player

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void onJoin(ServerPlayer player);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called after player a has been respawned

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void onLeave(ServerPlayer player);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called when a player has joined the game

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowDeath(ServerPlayer player, DamageSource damageSource, float damageAmount);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Deprecated: Use the more general ALLOW_DEATH event instead and check for instanceof ServerPlayer.

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowAdd(MobEffectInstance effectInstance, LivingEntity entity, EffectEventContext ctx);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: An event that checks whether an effect may be added. This event can be used to cancel effects given specific conditions such as a held item, a AttachmentType data attachment, or another status effect

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void beforeAdd(MobEffectInstance effectInstance, LivingEntity entity, EffectEventContext ctx);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether an effect may be added

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterAdd(MobEffectInstance effectInstance, LivingEntity entity, EffectEventContext ctx);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called before an effect is added

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: boolean allowEarlyRemove(MobEffectInstance effectInstance, LivingEntity entity, EffectEventContext ctx);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called after an effect is added

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void beforeRemove(MobEffectInstance effectInstance, LivingEntity entity, EffectEventContext ctx);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Checks whether an effect may be removed early. Note: this event is not called when an effect expires. The behavior of effect expiry typically should not be modified

fabric-entity-events-v1.function: void afterRemove(MobEffectInstance effectInstance, LivingEntity entity, EffectEventContext ctx);
fabric-entity-events-v1.description: Called before an effect is removed

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: void afterBlockBreak(ClientLevel level, LocalPlayer player, BlockPos pos, BlockState state);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback after a block is broken client side. Only called client side. For server side see AFTER

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: boolean onClientPlayerPreAttack(Minecraft client, LocalPlayer player, int clickCount);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: This event fires every tick when the attack key (left mouse button by default) is pressed (including clicking and holding the attack key). If the callback returns true, the vanilla handling (block breaking, entity attacking, hand swing) will be cancelled, and the later callbacks of this event are also cancelled. This event is client-only, which means handling it may require sending custom packets. The event fires both when clicking and holding attack key. To check whether the attack key is just clicked, use clickCount != 0 The vanilla attack cooldown and player game mode does not affect this event. The mod probably needs to check missTime and the game mode. ItemCooldowns can be used for custom item cooldown handling

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: InteractionResult interact(Player player, Level level, InteractionHand hand, BlockPos pos, Direction direction);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback for left-clicking ("attacking") a block. Is hooked in before the spectator check, so make sure to check for the player's game mode as well! On the logical client, the return values have the following meaning: SUCCESS cancels further processing, causes a hand swing, and sends a packet to the server. CONSUME cancels further processing, and sends a packet to the server. It does NOT cause a hand swing. PASS falls back to further processing. FAIL cancels further processing and does not send a packet to the server. On the logical server, the return values have the following meaning: PASS falls back to further processing. Any other value cancels further processing

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: InteractionResult interact(Player player, Level level, InteractionHand hand, Entity entity, @Nullable EntityHitResult hitResult);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback for left-clicking ("attacking") an entity. Is hooked in before the spectator check, so make sure to check for the player's game mode as well! Upon return: SUCCESS cancels further processing and, on the client, sends a packet to the server. PASS falls back to further processing. FAIL cancels further processing and does not send a packet to the server

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: boolean beforeBlockBreak(Level level, Player player, BlockPos pos, BlockState state, @Nullable BlockEntity blockEntity);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback before a block is broken. Only called on the server, however updates are synced with the client. If any listener cancels a block breaking action, that block breaking action is cancelled and CANCELED event is fired. Otherwise, the AFTER event is fired

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: void afterBlockBreak(Level level, Player player, BlockPos pos, BlockState state, @Nullable BlockEntity blockEntity);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Called before a block is broken and allows cancelling the block breaking. Implementations should not modify the level or assume the block break has completed or failed

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: void onBlockBreakCanceled(Level level, Player player, BlockPos pos, BlockState state, @Nullable BlockEntity blockEntity);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Called after a block is successfully broken

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: ItemStack onPickItemFromBlock(ServerPlayer player, BlockPos pos, BlockState state, boolean requestIncludeData);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Called when a player requests to pick the item for a block at a given position

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: ItemStack onPickItemFromEntity(ServerPlayer player, Entity entity, boolean requestIncludeData);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Determines the pick item stack to give to a player that is attempting to pick an item from a block

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: InteractionResult interact(Player player, Level level, InteractionHand hand, BlockHitResult hitResult);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback for right-clicking ("using") a block. Is hooked in before the spectator check, so make sure to check for the player's game mode as well! Upon return: SUCCESS cancels further processing and, on the client, sends a packet to the server. PASS falls back to further processing. FAIL cancels further processing and does not send a packet to the server

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: InteractionResult interact(Player player, Level level, InteractionHand hand, Entity entity, @Nullable EntityHitResult hitResult);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback for right-clicking ("using") an entity. Is hooked in before the spectator check, so make sure to check for the player's game mode as well! On the logical client, the return values have the following meaning: SUCCESS cancels further processing, causes a hand swing, and sends a packet to the server. CONSUME cancels further processing, and sends a packet to the server. It does NOT cause a hand swing. PASS falls back to further processing. FAIL cancels further processing and does not send a packet to the server. On the logical server, the return values have the following meaning: PASS falls back to further processing. Any other value cancels further processing. Note that on the server, the EntityHitResult may be null if the client successfully interacted using the interactOn(Entity, InteractionHand, Vec3) position-less overload}. On the client, the EntityHitResult will never be null

fabric-events-interaction-v0.function: InteractionResult interact(Player player, Level level, InteractionHand hand);
fabric-events-interaction-v0.description: Callback for right-clicking ("using") an item. Is hooked in before the spectator check, so make sure to check for the player's game mode as well! Upon return: SUCCESS cancels further processing and, on the client, sends a packet to the server. PASS falls back to further processing. FAIL cancels further processing and does not send a packet to the server

fabric-item-api-v1.function: void getTooltip(ItemStack stack, Item.TooltipContext tooltipContext, TooltipFlag tooltipFlag, List<Component> lines);
fabric-item-api-v1.description: Fired after the game has appended all base tooltip lines to the list
Modify the default data components of the specified item
Modify the default data components of the specified items

fabric-item-api-v1.function: void modify(DataComponentMap.Builder builder, HolderLookup.Provider lookupProvider, Item item);
fabric-item-api-v1.description: Modify the default data components of items using the provided ModifyContext instance

An event that allows overriding whether an Enchantment can be applied to an ItemStack. This should only be used to modify the behavior of external items with regards to external enchantments, where 'external' means either vanilla or from another mod. For instance, a mod might allow enchanting a pickaxe with Sharpness (and only Sharpness) under certain specific conditions. To modify the behavior of your own modded enchantments, specify a custom tag for supportedItems() instead. To modify the behavior of your own modded items, add to the applicable tags instead, when that suffices. Note that this event triggers before canBeEnchantedWith(ItemStack, Holder, EnchantingContext), and that method will only be called if no listeners override it. Note that allowing an enchantment using this event does not guarantee the item will receive that enchantment, only that it isn't forbidden from doing so
Checks whether an Enchantment should be applied to a given ItemStack

fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.description: Called when a BlockEntity is loaded into a ClientLevel. When this event is called, the block entity is already in the level. However, its data might not be loaded yet, so don't rely on it
Called when a BlockEntity is about to be unloaded from a ClientLevel. When this event is called, the block entity is still present on the level
Called when a chunk is loaded into a ClientLevel. When this event is called, the chunk is already in the level
Called when a chunk is about to be unloaded from a ClientLevel. When this event is called, the chunk is still present in the level
Called when an Entity is loaded into a ClientLevel. When this event is called, the chunk is already in the level
Called when an Entity is about to be unloaded from a ClientLevel. This event is called before the entity is unloaded from the level
An event which is called after the client level has been changed
Called when Minecraft has started and it's client about to tick for the first time. This occurs while the splash screen is displayed
Called when Minecraft's client begins to stop. This is caused by quitting while in game, or closing the game window. This will be called before the integrated server is stopped if it is running
Called at the start of the client tick
Called at the end of the client tick
Called at the start of a ClientLevel's tick
Called at the end of a ClientLevel's tick. End of level tick may be used to start async computations for the next tick
Called when tags are loaded or updated
Called when an BlockEntity is loaded into a ServerLevel. When this is event is called, the block entity is already in the level. However, its data might not be loaded yet, so don't rely on it
Called when an BlockEntity is about to be unloaded from a ServerLevel. When this event is called, the block entity is still present on the level

fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.function: void onChunkLoad(ServerLevel level, LevelChunk chunk, boolean generated);
fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.description: Called when a chunk is loaded into a ServerLevel. When this event is called, the chunk is already in the level. Note that this event is not called for chunks that become accessible without previously being unloaded

Called when a newly generated chunk is loaded into a ServerLevel. When this event is called, the chunk is already in the level
Deprecated: use CHUNK_LOAD directly instead

Called when a chunk is unloaded from a ServerLevel. When this event is called, the chunk is still present in the level. Note that the server typically unloads chunks when the chunk's load level goes above MAX_LEVEL (and not immediately when the chunk becomes inaccessible). To know when a chunk first becomes inaccessible, see FULL_CHUNK_STATUS_CHANGE

fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.function: void onFullChunkStatusChange(ServerLevel level, LevelChunk chunk, FullChunkStatus oldChunkStatus, FullChunkStatus newChunkStatus);
fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.description: Called when a chunk's actual ticking behavior is about to align with its updated FullChunkStatus. When this event is being called: The chunk's getFullStatus() has already changed. Entities within the chunk are not guaranteed to be accessible. The chunk's corresponding full chunk status future in ChunkHolder is not guaranteed to be done. When transitioning from INACCESSIBLE to FULL, calling getChunkFuture(int, int, ChunkStatus, boolean) to fetch the current chunk at FULL status results in undefined behavior
Called when an Entity is loaded into a ServerLevel. When this event is called, the entity is already in the level
Called when an Entity is unloaded from a ServerLevel. This event is called before the entity is removed from the level

fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.function: void onChange(LivingEntity livingEntity, EquipmentSlot equipmentSlot, ItemStack previousStack, ItemStack currentStack);
fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.description: Called during tick() if the Entity's equipment has been changed or mutated. This event is also called when the entity joins the level. A change in equipment is determined by matches(ItemStack, ItemStack)
Called just after a level is loaded by a Minecraft server. This can be used to load level specific metadata or initialize a SavedData on a server level

Called before a level is unloaded by a Minecraft server. This typically occurs after a server has SERVER_STOPPING started shutting down. Mods which allow dynamic level (un)registration should call this event so mods can let go of level handles when a level is removed
Called when a Minecraft server is starting. This occurs before the PlayerList player list and any levels are loaded
Called when a Minecraft server has started and is about to tick for the first time. At this stage, all levels are live

Called when a Minecraft server has started shutting down. This occurs before the server's network channel is closed and before any players are disconnected. For example, an integrated server will begin stopping, but its client may continue to run. All levels are still present and can be modified

Called when a Minecraft server has stopped. All levels have been closed and all (block)entities and players have been unloaded. For example, an CLIENT integrated server will begin stopping, but its client may continue to run. Meanwhile, for a SERVER dedicated server, this will be the last event called
Called when a Minecraft server is about to send tag and recipe data to a player

Called right before tags and recipes are sent to a player, either because the player joined, or because the server reloaded resources. The getResourceManager() server resource manager is up-to-date when this is called. For example, this event can be used to sync data loaded with custom resource reloaders

fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.function: void endDataPackReload(MinecraftServer server, CloseableResourceManager resourceManager, boolean success);
fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.description: Called after a Minecraft server has reloaded data packs. If reloading data packs was unsuccessful, the current data packs will be kept

fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.function: void onBeforeSave(MinecraftServer server, boolean flush, boolean force);
fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.description: Called after data packs on a Minecraft server have been reloaded. If the reload was not successful, the old data packs will be kept

fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.function: void onAfterSave(MinecraftServer server, boolean flush, boolean force);
fabric-lifecycle-events-v1.description: Called before a Minecraft server begins saving data
Called at the start of the server tick. When the dedicated server is "paused", this event is not invoked
Called at the end of the server tick. When the dedicated server is "paused", this event is not invoked
Called at the start of a ServerLevel's tick. When the dedicated server is "paused", this event is not invoked
Called at the end of a ServerLevel's tick. End of level tick may be used to start async computations for the next tick. When the dedicated server is "paused", this event is not invoked

fabric-loot-api-v3.function: LootTable replaceLootTable(ResourceKey<LootTable> key, LootTable original, LootTableSource source, HolderLookup.Provider holder);
fabric-loot-api-v3.description: This event can be used to replace loot tables. If a loot table is replaced, the iteration will stop for that loot table

fabric-loot-api-v3.function: void modifyLootTable(ResourceKey<LootTable> key, LootTable.Builder tableBuilder, LootTableSource source, HolderLookup.Provider holder);

fabric-loot-api-v3.description: Called when a loot table is loading to modify loot tables

fabric-loot-api-v3.function: void modifyLootTableDrops(Holder<LootTable> holder, LootContext context, List<ItemStack> drops);
fabric-loot-api-v3.description: Called when all loot tables have been loaded and REPLACE and MODIFY have been invoked

fabric-message-api-v1.function: boolean allowReceiveChatMessage(Component message, @Nullable PlayerChatMessage playerChatMessage, @Nullable GameProfile sender, ChatType.Bound boundChatType, Instant timeStamp);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: An event triggered when the client receives a chat message, which is any message sent by a player. Mods can use this to block the message. If a listener returned false, the message will not be displayed, the remaining listeners will be called (if any), and CHAT_CANCELED will be triggered instead of CHAT
Called when the client receives a chat message, which is any message sent by a player. Returning false prevents the message from being displayed, and CHAT_CANCELED will be triggered instead of CHAT

Called when the client receives a game message, which is any message sent by the server. Returning false prevents the message from being displayed, and GAME_CANCELED will be triggered instead of MODIFY_GAME. Overlay is whether the message will be displayed in the action bar. To toggle overlay, return false and call either sendSystemMessage(Component) LocalPlayer.sendSystemMessage(message) or sendOverlayMessage(Component) LocalPlayer.sendOverlayMessage(message)

fabric-message-api-v1.function: void onReceiveChatMessage(Component message, @Nullable PlayerChatMessage playerChatMessage, @Nullable GameProfile sender, ChatType.Bound boundChatType, Instant timeStamp);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the client receives a game message, which is any message sent by the server. Is not called when ALLOW_GAME game messages are blocked. Use GAME if not modifying the message. Overlay is whether the message will be displayed in the action bar. Use ALLOW_GAME to toggle overlay
Called when the client receives a chat message, which is any message sent by a player. Is not called when ALLOW_CHAT chat messages are blocked

fabric-message-api-v1.function: void onReceiveChatMessageCanceled(Component message, @Nullable PlayerChatMessage playerChatMessage, @Nullable GameProfile sender, ChatType.Bound boundChatType, Instant timeStamp);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the client receives a game message, which is any message sent by the server. Is not called when ALLOW_GAME game messages are blocked. Overlay is whether the message will be displayed in the action bar. Use ALLOW_GAME to toggle overlay
Called when receiving a chat message is canceled with ALLOW_CHAT

An event triggered when the client is about to send a chat message, typically from a client GUI. Mods can use this to block the message. If a listener returned false, the message will not be sent, the remaining listeners will not be called (if any), and CHAT_CANCELED will be triggered instead of MODIFY_CHAT
Called when the client is about to send a chat message, typically from a client GUI. Returning false prevents the message from being sent, and CHAT_CANCELED will be triggered instead of MODIFY_CHAT

Called when the client is about to send a command, which is whenever the player executes a command including client commands registered with fabric-command-api. Returning false prevents the command from being sent, and COMMAND_CANCELED will be triggered instead of MODIFY_COMMAND. The command string does not include a slash at the beginning
Called when the client sends a chat message, typically from a client GUI. Is not called when ALLOW_CHAT chat messages are blocked. Use CHAT if not modifying the message

Called when the client sends a command, which is whenever the player executes a command including client commands registered with fabric-command-api. Is not called when ALLOW_COMMAND command messages are blocked. The command string does not include a slash at the beginning. Use COMMAND if not modifying the command
Called when the client sends a chat message, typically from a client GUI. Is not called when ALLOW_CHAT chat messages are blocked

Called when the client sends a command, which is whenever the player executes a command including client commands registered with fabric-command-api. Is not called when ALLOW_COMMAND command messages are blocked. The command string does not include a slash at the beginning
Called when sending a chat message is canceled with ALLOW_CHAT

fabric-message-api-v1.function: boolean allowChatMessage(PlayerChatMessage message, ServerPlayer sender, ChatType.Bound boundChatType);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: An event triggered when the server broadcasts a chat message sent by a player, typically from a client GUI or a player-executed command. Mods can use this to block the message. If a listener returned false, the message will not be broadcast, the remaining listeners will not be called (if any), and CHAT_MESSAGE event will not be triggered. If the message is from a player-executed command, this will be called only if ALLOW_COMMAND_MESSAGE event did not block the message, and after triggering COMMAND_MESSAGE event

fabric-message-api-v1.function: boolean allowGameMessage(MinecraftServer server, Component message, boolean overlay);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the server broadcasts a chat message sent by a player, typically from a client GUI or a player-executed command. Returning false prevents the message from being broadcast and the CHAT_MESSAGE event from triggering. If the message is from a player-executed command, this will be called only if ALLOW_COMMAND_MESSAGE event did not block the message, and after triggering COMMAND_MESSAGE event

fabric-message-api-v1.function: boolean allowCommandMessage(PlayerChatMessage message, CommandSourceStack source, ChatType.Bound boundChatType);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the server broadcasts a game message to all players. Game messages include death messages, join/leave messages, and advancement messages. Returning false prevents the message from being broadcast and the GAME_MESSAGE event from triggering

fabric-message-api-v1.function: void onChatMessage(PlayerChatMessage message, ServerPlayer sender, ChatType.Bound boundChatType);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the server broadcasts a command message to all players, such as one from /me and /say (but not ones that specify the recipients like /msg). Returning false prevents the message from being broadcast and the COMMAND_MESSAGE event from triggering. If the command is executed by a player and the message is not blocked, ALLOW_CHAT_MESSAGE and CHAT_MESSAGE events will also be triggered after triggering COMMAND_MESSAGE

fabric-message-api-v1.function: void onGameMessage(MinecraftServer server, Component message, boolean overlay);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the server broadcasts a chat message sent by a player, typically from a client GUI or a player-executed command. Is not called when ALLOW_CHAT_MESSAGE chat messages are blocked. If the message is from a player-executed command, this will be called only if ALLOW_COMMAND_MESSAGE event did not block the message, and after triggering COMMAND_MESSAGE event

fabric-message-api-v1.function: void onCommandMessage(PlayerChatMessage message, CommandSourceStack source, ChatType.Bound boundChatType);
fabric-message-api-v1.description: Called when the server broadcasts a game message to all players. Game messages include death messages, join/leave messages, and advancement messages. Is not called when ALLOW_GAME_MESSAGE game messages are blocked

fabric-networking-api-v1.description: Event indicating a connection entering the CONFIGURATION state, ready for registering channel handlers. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
An event called after the connection has been initialized and is ready to start sending and receiving configuration packets. Packets may be sent during this event
An event called after the ClientboundFinishConfigurationPacket has been received, just before switching to the PLAY state. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
An event for the disconnection of the client configuration packet listener. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
Deprecated: replaced by Complete

Event indicating a connection entered the LOGIN state, ready for registering query request handlers. This event may be used by mods to prepare their client side state. This event does not guarantee that a login attempt will be successful

An event for when the client has started receiving login queries. A client can only start receiving login queries when a server has sent the first login query. Vanilla servers will typically never make the client enter this login phase, but it is not a guarantee that the connected server is a vanilla server since a modded server or proxy may have no login queries to send to the client and therefore bypass the login query phase. If this event is fired then it is a sign that a server is not a vanilla server or the server is behind a proxy which is capable of handling login queries. This event may be used to LoginQueryRequestHandler register login query handlers which may be used to send a response to a server. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
An event for when the client's login process has ended due to disconnection. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
Event indicating a connection entered the PLAY state, ready for registering channel handlers

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onPlayReady(ClientPacketListener listener, PacketSender sender, Minecraft client);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for notification when the client play packet listener is ready to send packets to the server. At this stage, the packet listener is ready to send packets to the server. Since the client's local state has been set up
An event for the disconnection of the client play packet listener. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelRegister(ClientConfigurationPacketListenerImpl listener, PacketSender sender, Minecraft client, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the client configuration packet listener receiving an update indicating the connected server's ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelUnregister(ClientConfigurationPacketListenerImpl listener, PacketSender sender, Minecraft client, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the client configuration packet listener receiving an update indicating the connected server's lack of ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelRegister(ClientPacketListener listener, PacketSender sender, Minecraft client, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the client play packet listener receiving an update indicating the connected server's ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelUnregister(ClientPacketListener listener, PacketSender sender, Minecraft client, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the client play packet listener receiving an update indicating the connected server's lack of ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelRegister(ServerConfigurationPacketListenerImpl listener, PacketSender sender, MinecraftServer server, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the server configuration packet listener receiving an update indicating the connected client's ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelUnregister(ServerConfigurationPacketListenerImpl listener, PacketSender sender, MinecraftServer server, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the server configuration packet listener receiving an update indicating the connected client's lack of ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelRegister(ServerGamePacketListenerImpl listener, PacketSender sender, MinecraftServer server, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the server game packet listener receiving an update indicating the connected client's ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onChannelUnregister(ServerGamePacketListenerImpl listener, PacketSender sender, MinecraftServer server, List<Identifier> channels);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the server game packet listener receiving an update indicating the connected client's lack of ability to receive packets in certain channels. This event may be invoked at any time after login and up to disconnection

An event that is called after a player has started tracking an entity. Typically, this occurs when an entity enters a client's view distance. This event is called after the entity's getAddEntityPacket(ServerEntity) spawn packet is sent to the player
Called after a player has started tracking an entity
An event for the disconnection of the server configuration packet listener. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
Event indicating a connection entered the LOGIN state, ready for registering query response handlers

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onLoginStart(ServerLoginPacketListenerImpl listener, MinecraftServer server, LoginPacketSender sender, ServerLoginNetworking.LoginSynchronizer synchronizer);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for the start of login queries of the server login packet listener. This event may be used to register LoginQueryResponseHandler login query response handlers using registerReceiver(ServerLoginPacketListenerImpl, Identifier, ServerLoginNetworking.LoginQueryResponseHandler) since this event is fired just before the first login query response is processed. You may send login queries to the connected client using the provided LoginPacketSender
An event for the disconnection of the server login packet listener. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked
Event indicating a connection entered the PLAY state, ready for registering channel handlers

fabric-networking-api-v1.function: void onPlayReady(ServerGamePacketListenerImpl listener, PacketSender sender, MinecraftServer server);
fabric-networking-api-v1.description: An event for notification when the server game packet listener is ready to send packets to the client. At this stage, the packet listener is ready to send packets to the client
An event for the disconnection of the server game packet listener. No packets should be sent when this event is invoked

fabric-particles-v1.function: boolean allowTerrainParticleTint(BlockState state, ClientLevel level, BlockPos pos);
fabric-particles-v1.description: An event that checks if a TerrainParticle terrain particle can be tinted using the corresponding block's BlockTintSource. The default return value of this event is true. If any callback returns false for a given call, further iteration will be canceled and the event invoker will return false

fabric-recipe-api-v1.description: This event gets invoked when client receives all synchronized recipes. It will only be invoked if the server client is currently connected supports Fabric's Recipe Sync packets

fabric-rendering-v1.description: Allows registering a mapping from TooltipComponent to ClientTooltipComponent. This allows custom tooltips for items: first, override getTooltipImage and return a custom TooltipComponent. Second, register a listener to this event and convert the component to your client component implementation if it's an instance of your component class. Note that failure to map some components to a client component will throw an exception, so make sure that any components you return in getTooltipImage will be handled by one of the callbacks

fabric-rendering-v1.function: void onExtractItemDecorations(GuiGraphicsExtractor graphics, Font font, ItemStack stack, int x, int y);
fabric-rendering-v1.description: Fires at the end of itemDecorations(Font, ItemStack, int, int, String) and allows for drawing custom item stack decorations. In vanilla these are: durability bar, cooldown overlay and stack count

Called when the level renderer reloads, usually as result of changing resource pack or video configuration, or when the player types F3+A in the debug screen. Afterwards all render chunks will be reset and reloaded. Render chunks and other render-related object instances will be made null or invalid after this event so do not use it to capture dependent state. Instead, use it to invalidate state and reinitialize lazily
An event that can prevent capes from rendering

fabric-rendering-v1.function: void registerLayers(EntityType<? extends LivingEntity> entityType, LivingEntityRenderer<?, ?, ?> entityRenderer, RegistrationHelper registrationHelper, EntityRendererProvider.Context context);

fabric-rendering-v1.description: Called after the block outline render state is extracted, before it is drawn. Can optionally cancel the default rendering by setting the outline render state to null but all handlers for this event will always be called. Use this to extract custom data needed when decorating or replacing the default block outline rendering for specific modded blocks or when normally, the block outline would not be extracted to be rendered. Normally, outline rendering will not happen for entities, fluids, or other game objects that do not register a block-type hit. To attach modded data to vanilla render states, see FabricRenderState FabricRenderState. Only attach the minimum data needed for rendering. Do not attach objects that are not thread-safe such as ClientLevel. Setting the outline render state to null by any event subscriber will cancel the default block outline render and suppress the BEFORE_BLOCK_OUTLINE event. This has no effect on other subscribers to this event - all subscribers will always be called. Setting outline render state to null here is appropriate when there is still a valid block hit (with a fluid, for example) and you don't want the block outline render to appear. This event should NOT be used for general-purpose replacement of the default block outline rendering because it will interfere with mod-specific renders. Mods that replace the default block outline for specific blocks should instead subscribe to BEFORE_BLOCK_OUTLINE

Called after all render states are extracted, before any are drawn. Use this to extract general custom data needed for rendering. To attach modded data to vanilla render states, see FabricRenderState FabricRenderState. Only attach the minimum data needed for rendering. Do not attach objects that are not thread-safe such as ClientLevel

Called at the start of the main pass, after the sky is drawn to the appropriate framebuffers and all chunks to be rendered are uploaded to GPU, and before any chunks are drawn to the appropriate framebuffers

Called after OPAQUE opaque terrain is drawn to the appropriate framebuffers, and before any submit nodes are added to the submit node storage. Use this event to render additional opaque terrain-like geometry

Called after OPAQUE opaque terrain is drawn to the appropriate framebuffers and all submit nodes from entities, block entities, and particles are added to the submit node storage, and before any submit geometry is drawn to the appropriate framebuffers. Use this event to add additional submits to submitNodeCollector()
Called after the solid geometry of submits collected from entities, block entities, and particles are drawn to the appropriate framebuffers

Called after the translucent geometry of submits collected from entities and block entities are drawn to the appropriate framebuffers. Note that this excludes translucent particle geometry, which is rendered much later

Called after block outline render checks are made and before the default block outline is drawn to the appropriate framebuffers. This will NOT be called if the default outline render state was set to null in AFTER_BLOCK_OUTLINE_EXTRACTION. Use this to replace the default block outline rendering for specific blocks that need special outline rendering or to add information that doesn't replace the block outline. Subscribers cannot affect each other or detect if another subscriber is also handling a specific block. If two subscribers render for the same block, both renders will appear. Returning false from any event subscriber will cancel the default block outline render. This has no effect on other subscribers to this event - all subscribers will always be called. Canceling is appropriate when the subscriber replacing the default block outline render for a specific block

Called after all geometry of submits collected from entities, block entities, and particles (except translucent particle geometry), the block breaking overlay, and the block outline for solid blocks are drawn to the appropriate framebuffers, and before gizmos are collected

Called after opaque terrain, entities, block entities, solid particles, overlays, and gizmos are drawn to the appropriate framebuffers, and before TRANSLUCENT translucent terrain and translucent particles are drawn to the appropriate framebuffers

Called after terrain, entities, block entities, solid particles, overlays, and gizmos are drawn to the appropriate framebuffers, and before translucent particles are drawn to the appropriate framebuffers. Use this event to render additional translucent terrain-like geometry

Called at the end of the main render pass, after terrain, entities, block entities, and particles are drawn to the appropriate framebuffers, and before clouds, weather, and late debug are drawn to the appropriate framebuffers and before fabulous translucent framebuffers are combined. Warning: after rendering things in this event, consumers should call endBatch() context.bufferSource().endBatch(), otherwise you may get strange rendering bugs!

fabric-screen-api-v1.function: void beforeInit(Minecraft client, Screen screen, int scaledWidth, int scaledHeight);
void afterInit(Minecraft client, Screen screen, int scaledWidth, int scaledHeight);

fabric-screen-api-v1.description: An event that is called after removed() is called. This event signifies that the screen is now closed. This event is typically used to undo any screen specific state changes or to terminate threads spawned by a screen. This event may precede initialization events BEFORE_INIT but there is no guarantee that event will be called immediately afterwards

fabric-screen-api-v1.function: void beforeExtract(Screen screen, GuiGraphicsExtractor graphics, int mouseX, int mouseY, float tickProgress);
fabric-screen-api-v1.description: An event that is called before a screen is extracted

fabric-screen-api-v1.function: void afterBackground(Screen screen, GuiGraphicsExtractor graphics, int mouseX, int mouseY, float tickProgress);
fabric-screen-api-v1.description: An event that is called after a screen's background is extracted

fabric-screen-api-v1.function: void afterExtract(Screen screen, GuiGraphicsExtractor graphics, int mouseX, int mouseY, float tickProgress);
fabric-screen-api-v1.description: An event that is called after a screen is extracted
An event that is called before a screen is ticked
An event that is called after a screen is ticked
